business.headline: Why many Indians earning Rs 1 lakh still struggle to saveFor many Indians, earning Rs 1 lakh a month once symbolised financial success. Yet, despite reaching this milestone, many professionals find themselves living pay cheque to pay cheque, with little left for savings. So where does all the money go?

Sebi looks to widen direct trading access; retail investors could benefitSebi has proposed allowing all categories of investors, including retail participants, to use Direct Market Access (DMA), a facility that enables orders to be placed directly into a stock exchange's trading system through a broker's infrastructure.

Sensex ends 893 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; Infosys falls 3%The S&P BSE Sensex dropped 893.39 points, or 1.16%, to close at 76,200.68, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 278.80 points, or 1.16%, to settle at 23,824.10.

Sensex drops over 900 points: Why is stock market falling today?Dalal Street fell sharply on Tuesday as an Asian market selloff and weak IT shares dragged benchmarks lower. The decline has shifted focus to US rate signals, monsoon worries and fading momentum after the recent rally.

Got your Form 16? Here's why Form 130 won't affect your ITR filing this yearConfused about Form 16 and Form 130? If you're preparing to file your ITR, here's why the upcoming change won't affect your return this year.

Iran wants to sell oil to India after US waiver. Tankers are nearby, but will India buy?Iran has begun pitching crude oil to Indian and other Asian refiners after the US waived sanctions for 60 days amid peace-deal negotiations. With Iranian tankers close to Indian shores, New Delhi appears to be an attractive market for Tehran, but will Indian refiners return to buying Iranian oil?

Pension complaint? Here are 5 ways PFRDA's new AI-powered platform can help youThe Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority has launched a new AI-powered platform called PFRDA Pension Sahayak, aimed at making pension grievance redressal faster, simpler and more accessible.

Seoul's Kospi crashes 10%, Japan's Nikkei falls 4%. Why are Asian markets down?Asian markets tumbled as investors sold tech and AI stocks, with South Korea's Kospi plunging 10% and Japan's Nikkei falling 3.6%.

Expecting an ITR deadline extension? Here's why it may not happen this yearMany taxpayers are hoping for another extension to the ITR filing deadline, as seen in previous years. However, this year's filing season appears different, and the chances of additional time may be lower than expected.

India-US trade talks enter final stretch ahead of July 24 deadlineUnion Minister Piyush Goyal and US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer are meeting in New Delhi to finalise the first phase of the India-US trade pact. The talks are significant as both sides seek tariff gains before the July 24 deadline.

SpaceX gets a reality check days after historic IPO listing, loses $600 billionLess than two weeks after making history, SpaceX is already confronting the burden of expectation. Investors have embraced Elon Musk's vision; now they want evidence that the business can deliver.

Sensex opens 170 points lower; Nifty tops 24,000; Infosys down 3%The S&P BSE Sensex fell 77.92 points, or 0.10%, to 77,016.15 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 18.90 points, or 0.08%, to 24,083.35.

SpaceX loses $600bn in 3 days, nearly 3 times Ambani and Adani's combined wealthThe wipeout is nearly three times the combined wealth of India's two richest businessmen — Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani, whose net worth stands at $88.3 billion, and Adani Group Chairman Gautam Adani, whose fortune is estimated at $120 billion.

Meta, CRED and Kunal Shah: What's behind the $900 million deal?Meta's investment in CRED and its founder Kunal Shah has renewed interest in the fintech company's journey, business model and what lies ahead for one of India's most closely watched startups.

Tata Electronics hit by cyberattack; 2 lakh files linked to Apple, Tesla leakedThe breach is the latest setback for Apple's supply chain in India, where Tata faces scrutiny over alleged contamination of farmlands near one of its iPhone parts plants, Reuters reported.

8th Pay Commission in Lucknow: How much could salaries and pensions increase?With the 8th Pay Commission holding fresh talks in Lucknow, expectations around pay revisions and pension increases are once again in focus. The discussions could help shape the recommendations that follow.

Silver jumps Rs 5,000, gold up Rs 2,000: Time to buy or sell?Silver and gold prices have surged sharply today, sparking fresh interest among investors after recent volatility. So is this rally a buying opportunity or time to book profits?

Sensex closes 291 points higher, Nifty above 24,100; RIL up 1%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 291.17 points, or 0.38%, to close at 77,094.07, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 89.80 points, or 0.37%, to settle at 24,102.90.

India's economy is booming. So why are foreign investors looking away?For years, India rolled out the red carpet for foreign investors, citing its vast market, consumer base and ease of doing business. Now, as global capital chases artificial intelligence and semiconductors, those strengths no longer seem enough.

8th Pay Commission: What pensioners and family pension beneficiaries can expectThe 8th Pay Commission has begun consultations in Lucknow with unions, associations and department representatives. Its recommendations could shape pensions, family pensions and retirement security for millions.

entertainment.headline: Naseeruddin Shah's Main Vaapas Aaunga era: 75, fearless, unmatched Naseeruddin Shah doesn't chase relevance; he defines it by showing up fully for stories that matter — poetic and personal. He quietly schools an entire industry on what sustained excellence looks like: fearless, and forever in demand. A glimpse at his versatility in Gustaakh Ishq, Made in India: A Titan Story and Main Vaapas Aaunga.

Deepika Padukone calls ICC's maternity policy a 'landmark moment' Deepika Padukone welcomed the ICC's new post-pregnancy guidelines for female cricketers. The actor is currently pregnant with her second child with husband, actor Ranveer Singh.

Alliance full contestants list: Daisy Shah, Ravi Kishan and all confirmed names Let the ultimate game of trust, power, and survival begin! Here's a list of the confirmed celebrities who are set to join Prime Video's upcoming show Alliance.

Nafisa Ali treks at 11,000 feet with familly and friends amid cancer recovery. Video Veteran actor Nafisa Ali Sodhi has shared videos from a trek at 11,000 feet with family and friends amid her cancer battle. The clips have renewed attention on her recovery journey and her earlier account of a delayed diagnosis.

Alka Yagnik gets love from fans as Padma Bhushan appearance sparks health concerns Alka Yagnik received the Padma Bhushan from President Droupadi Murmu at Rashtrapati Bhavan on June 23. The ceremony video prompted fans to send wishes as concern grew over her frail appearance.

Vijay makes 'it all worth it': Trisha's love-filled birthday post for CM After leaving fans wondering with her silence on June 22, actor Trisha Krishnan has finally posted a birthday note for Vijay. Sharing a heartfelt picture from the Tamil Nadu Chief Minister's late-night birthday celebrations, the actor's post comes amid rumours that the two had parted ways.

Meet Sukrut Deo: The breakout star of Latent 2 praised by Alia Bhatt, Samay Raina Comedian Sukrut Deo turned heads on India's Got Latent Season 2 with a viral act that earned praise from actor Alia Bhatt and show creator Samay Raina. His performance and emotional on-stage moments also resulted in a surge in his social media followers.

Eetha teaser X reactions: Fans crown Shraddha the face of women-led cinema The teaser of Eetha offers a first look at Shraddha Kapoor as Vithabai Bhau Mang Narayangaonkar. Fans reacted to the teaser and praised the film's rooted portrayal of Maharashtra's folk arts.

Appa was very angry: Shruti Haasan on her teenage act that angered Kamal Haasan Shruti Haasan revealed she has not visited a hair salon in five years and recalled how Kamal Haasan was "very, very angry" when she got a belly button piercing at 19.

Padma Awards 2026: Mammootty, Alka Yagnik to Madhavan, complete list of winners President Droupadi Murmu conferred actors Mammootty, R Madhavan and singer Alka Yagnik at the Padma Awards 2026 on Tuesday. The ceremony was held at the second Civil Investiture Ceremony in New Delhi.

Did Ram Charan review Dhurandhar before watching it? Here's why fans are confused Ram Charan recently revealed that he watched Dhurandhar on OTT last week and called it a terrific film. However, the actor had already shared a detailed review praising Dhurandhar: The Revenge during its theatrical release in March.

Main Vaapas Aaunga actor Anjana Sukhani calls Naseeruddin Shah a 'live masterclass' Actor Anjana Sukhani praised actor Naseeruddin Shah, calling him a "live masterclass" and reflecting on the invaluable experience of working with him in Main Vaapas Aaunga. She said sharing screen space with the acclaimed actor was both inspiring and a learning opportunity.

Did Trisha unfollow CM Vijay on Instagram? Here's what we know Fresh speculation has erupted online after reports claimed that Trisha unfollowed Vijay on Instagram, though neither actor has confirmed the alleged social media move.

Blossom, Bubbles and Buttercup return in new Powerpuff Girls film Warner Bros Pictures Animation has put a new Powerpuff Girls film into development. The move marks another effort to bring the long-running franchise back to cinemas.

Ram Kapoor hugs Ektaa Kapoor, addresses rift as he joins Lock Upp 2 Actor Ram Kapoor addressed speculation about his equation with Ektaa Kapoor at the launch of Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa. He is set to enter the show as a contestant.

Chiranjeevi congratulates Samantha-Raj on 'new chapter', praises Maa Inti Bangaaram Chiranjeevi praised Maa Inti Bangaaram on social media and wished Samantha Ruth Prabhu and Raj Nidimoru well. His message underlined the film's women-led storytelling as it continued a strong box office run.

Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram's dream run continues, hits Rs 50 crore milestone Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram has crossed Rs 50 crore worldwide in five days. The film's run has drawn industry praise and renewed attention to female-led Telugu stories.

Not going to be play doll: Actor Shwetha Menon feels relieved after film body exit Shweta Menon and the entire AMMA executive committee resigned after a no-confidence move at the annual meeting. Her exit has sharpened the row over accounts, internal dissent and control of the actors' body.

Director Vikram Bhatt compares Obsession to his 2002 horror film Raaz Director Vikram Bhatt has said that the Hollywood horror film Obsession reminded him of his 2002 supernatural film. He said horror works best when fear feels believable rather than driven by star power.

general news.headline: Pune murder: 2 attempts, fiancée's birthday picnic ruse, and a mournful Insta postA cancelled Bali trip, an alleged failed murder attempt, a birthday picnic at a historic fort and a grieving Instagram post. Investigators say each piece formed part of a chilling plot that ended in death.

Pak's Rafale loss claims busted again, Air Force document shows all 36 in serviceAn Indian Air Force tender seeking bridge support for all 36 Rafale fighter jets contradicts Pakistan's repeated claims that India lost multiple Rafales during Operation Sindoor.

Churchill starved millions of Indians to death. Artwork on it riles Britishers nowAn installation at a London gallery which described former British PM Winston Churchill as responsible for the "wilful starvation" of millions of Indians during the 1943 Bengal Famine, has been removed from the public display. The artist was forced to take the installation off after at least 50 members of the House of Lords, including Chirchill's grandson, protested and demanded its removal.

Iran wants to sell oil to India after US waiver. Tankers are nearby, but will India buy?Iran has begun pitching crude oil to Indian and other Asian refiners after the US waived sanctions for 60 days amid peace-deal negotiations. With Iranian tankers close to Indian shores, New Delhi appears to be an attractive market for Tehran, but will Indian refiners return to buying Iranian oil?

Vijay makes 'it all worth it': Trisha's love-filled birthday post for CMAfter leaving fans wondering with her silence on June 22, actor Trisha Krishnan has finally posted a birthday note for Vijay. Sharing a heartfelt picture from the Tamil Nadu Chief Minister's late-night birthday celebrations, the actor's post comes amid rumours that the two had parted ways.

POR vs UZB Live: Can Ronaldo silence the 'finished' buzz?Portugal (POR) vs Uzbekistan (UZB) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal have arrived at a familiar crossroads after a frustrating World Cup start. Against Uzbekistan, they have a chance not only to silence the critics, but also to rediscover the fluency that has so far been missing.

MP CM didn't buy land after taking office: Sources on suspicious land deal claimA report claimed that Madhya Pradesh Chief Minister Mohan Yadav's family acquired large tracts of land in Ujjain that were expected to benefit from upcoming government infrastructure projects.

Pune woman who pushed fiancé off fort had made similar attempt 4 days earlierPolice are now examining allegations that Ketan Agarwal survived a first attempt on his life at Lohagad Fort on June 14 before returning to the same spot on June 18, where investigators allege he was pushed to his death.

Is Pakistan using Indus Valley to trick world on Indus treaty shelved by India?Pakistan is embracing the Indus Valley Civilisation as part of its national identity in a big way. It's a notable shift for a country that held the Sindh conquest of Muhammad bin Qasim in 712 CE as its foundational moment. So, why is Pakistan suddenly in love with Harappa and Mohenjo-daro? Does it have to do with the Indus Waters Treaty, which India has kept in abeyance?

You left me on my birthday, come back: What Pune woman posted after killing fiancéBusinessman Ketan Agarwal's death was initially believed to be an accident. However, his fiancée's statements aroused suspicion and prompted the police to dig deeper into the matter.

Sanitary pad checks, bra inspections: NEET students share shocking frisking storiesAfter a Re-NEET candidate alleged she was asked to lower her pants to prove she was wearing a sanitary pad, dozens of students flooded social media comments with similar accounts. Their stories raise troubling questions about exam security, personal dignity, and the mental health impact of invasive frisking practices.

US student visas to come with expiry date soon. A worry for Indians?A proposed overhaul of the US student visa system could replace the decades-old Duration of Status framework with fixed periods of stay, forcing international students to seek government approval to continue their studies and stay. With Indians now the largest foreign student group in the US, the changes could have far-reaching consequences.

3 CBSE toppers from Jharkhand; Class 12 re-evaluation result rewrites merit listCBSE's Class 12 re-evaluation results pushed Jharkhand students to the top of Commerce, Science and Arts. The revised scores gave the state a first across all three streams and included Avani Kejriwal's perfect 500.

We conducted a fuel efficiency test of E85. Here's what we foundWith E85 fuel priced nearly Rs 20 lower per litre than E20, it appears to be a bargain. However, ethanol's lower energy density also means higher fuel consumption. So, does the lower pump price actually translate into lower running costs? We put the Suzuki Gixxer SF flex fuel through a mileage test to find out.

Padma Awards 2026: Mammootty, Alka Yagnik to Madhavan, complete list of winnersPresident Droupadi Murmu conferred actors Mammootty, R Madhavan and singer Alka Yagnik at the Padma Awards 2026 on Tuesday. The ceremony was held at the second Civil Investiture Ceremony in New Delhi.

Fresh spell of monsoon rain across west coast and northeast on WednesdayThe IMD has forecast an active monsoon and rains across several regions, while some parts will continue to battle rising temperatures as monsoon remains on the way on June 24.

Instant noodles are a guilty pleasure Millennials and Gen Z refuse to give upHealth-conscious Millennials and Gen Z in India continue to buy instant noodles despite knowing their nutritional drawbacks. The reason? Let's find out.

Pune man, who died while taking pic on fort, pushed by fiancée: CopsKetan Vishal Agarwal's death at Pune's Lohagad Fort, initially believed to be an accidental fall, is now being investigated as a suspected murder. Police suspect his fiancee and another man may have conspired to push him into the valley.

Pune woman, lover sent to 7-day police custody over fiancé's murder at fortPolice allege Ketan Agarwal was pushed to his death at Lohagad Fort after his fiancée initially claimed he had slipped. The two accused have confessed to the crime and remain in police custody.

Why many Indians earning Rs 1 lakh still struggle to saveFor many Indians, earning Rs 1 lakh a month once symbolised financial success. Yet, despite reaching this milestone, many professionals find themselves living pay cheque to pay cheque, with little left for savings. So where does all the money go?

Argentina make life easier for Messi. Portugal make it harder for RonaldoArgentina have built a World Cup-winning ecosystem around Lionel Messi, allowing him to thrive at 39. Portugal, despite possessing immense talent, are still searching for the right formula to maximise Cristiano Ronaldo as another crucial test awaits against Uzbekistan.

Students waited a month for NEET re-test. Couldn't they be spared 2 minutes, parents askThe NEET re-test gave the examination system a second chance after the paper leak controversy. But for some students, reaching minutes late because of rain, traffic, accidents or document confusion meant there was no second chance at all.

Prakash Raj faces non-bailable warrant over alleged multiple voter IDsA Bengaluru court has issued a non-bailable warrant against actor Prakash Raj. The order follows allegations that he holds voter identity cards in more than one state and raises the possibility of his arrest.

Idli, Indonesia and India: The 1000-year mystery behind a breakfast favouriteSomewhere in a news article, you may have read about the origin of the idli and its long association not with India, but with Indonesia. The moment such a claim is mentioned, it often raises eyebrows. So, what is the story of Idli? Let's explore it with this article.

sports.headline: POR vs UZB Live: Can Ronaldo silence the 'finished' buzz? Portugal (POR) vs Uzbekistan (UZB) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal have arrived at a familiar crossroads after a frustrating World Cup start. Against Uzbekistan, they have a chance not only to silence the critics, but also to rediscover the fluency that has so far been missing.

New Zealand survive Scotland scare, remain alive in semis race Women's T20 Wolrd CUp 2026: Izzy Sharp and Brooke Halliday revived New Zealand to chase 132 after an early wobble. Darcey Carter's unbeaten 72 proved insufficient as Scotland's semi-final hopes ended, keeping New Zealand alive.

IPL trade buzz: KKR, Rajasthan open talks with MI over potential Hardik Pandya move Kolkata Knight Riders and Rajasthan Royals have approached Mumbai Indians over a potential Hardik Pandya trade. Rajasthan are eyeing a swap deal involving opening batter Yashasvi Jaiswal.

IND vs PAK hockey live score: India beat Pakistan in 7-goal thriller India (IND) vs Pakistan (PAK) hockey live score, FIH Pro League: Pakistan bounced back with two late goals, but it was not enough as India won the seven-goal thriller 4-3 in London. Goals from Abhishek, Nilakanta Sharma, Sukhjeet Singh and Rajinder Singh sealed the win for India.

Rohit Sharma receives Padma Shri from President Murmu, wife Ritika beams with pride Indian cricketer Rohit Sharma, hockey star Savita Punia and tennis great Vijay Amritraj were among the sports stars who received their Padma Awards from President Droupadi Murmu on Tuesday in New Delhi.

Jordan knocked out of FIFA World Cup as resilient Algeria stage comeback victory FIFA World Cup: Jordan became the first team from Group J to be knocked out of the FIFA World Cup. Despite taking the lead in the first half, Jordan let it slip in two crucial moments and ended up losing 2-1 to Algeria.

Sree Charani becomes No. 1 T20I bowler a year after making international debut Sree Charani became the world's top-ranked T20I bowler just over a year after her India debut. The left-arm spinner has been in fine form in the ongoing Women's T20 World Cup campaign in England.

Argentina make life easier for Messi. Portugal make it harder for Ronaldo Argentina have built a World Cup-winning ecosystem around Lionel Messi, allowing him to thrive at 39. Portugal, despite possessing immense talent, are still searching for the right formula to maximise Cristiano Ronaldo as another crucial test awaits against Uzbekistan.

Promising Suryansh Shedge replaces Nitish Reddy for Ireland, England T20I series Suryansh Shedge has replaced injured Nitish Kumar Reddy in India's T20I squad for the Ireland and England series. The Mumbai all-rounder earns a maiden call-up after strong India A and domestic performances.

Rishabh Pant takes huge pay cut for Delhi Capitals return, Kuldeep joins LSG IPL 2027: Lucknow Super Giants have mutually parted ways with Rishabh Pant after a disastrous Indian Premier League season. Pant is set to return to his former franchise Delhi Capitals, having taken a huge pay cut.

From 1 to 100: Messi fans across generations steal the spotlight in World CupLionel Messi fans from two very different generations stole the spotlight on Monday, when Argentina took the field against Austria in the FIFA World Cup. A 100-year-old grandma, and a child, barely one-year-old, were seen celebrating Messi and Argentina in their group-stage game.

ODI queens, T20 learners: South Africa hold up the mirror to Harmanpreet's IndiaSouth Africa delivered a timely reality check to India in Manchester. The six-wicket defeat exposed glaring shortcomings in Harmanpreet Kaur's side and raised fresh questions about their ability to translate ODI dominance into T20 success as the battle for the semi-finals heats up.

After Hardik Pandya, all-rounder Nitish Reddy ruled out of UK tourIndia have been dealt a big blow ahead of their tour of the United Kingdom. All-rounder Nitish Reddy has been ruled out of the Ireland and England series due to a quadriceps injury.

Birth certificate says 15, big-match record says Sooryavanshi loves pressureTeenage prodigies arrive regularly in cricket. What sets Vaibhav Sooryavanshi apart, at least for now, is not just his talent but his growing habit of producing defining performances when the pressure peaks.

Dream fulfilled: Sooryavanshi speechless after seeing his name on India jerseyIndia's teenage batting star Vaibhav Sooryavanshi said he was left overwhelmed after seeing his name on an India jersey for the first time, calling it the fulfilment of a dream he had cherished since he first picked up a cricket bat.

Messi beware: France coach backs Mbappe to claim FIFA World Cup scoring crownFrance manager Didier Deschamps backed Kylian Mbappe to eventually surpass Lionel Messi and become the FIFA World Cup's all-time leading scorer after the France captain struck twice in a 3-0 win over Iraq on Monday.

Viking row: After taking over Times Square, Norway fans celebrate with HaalandErling Haaland and Norway's players performed the Viking row with supporters in New York after beating Senegal 3-2. Norway qualified from Group I alongside France into the knockouts round.

World Cup 2026: Haaland brace sends Norway into knockouts with 3-2 win over SenegalErling Haaland continued his prolific run at the FIFA World Cup 2026, scoring twice as Norway beat Senegal 3-2 in a thrilling Group I contest on Tuesday, June 23 (IST) to secure qualification for the Round of 32.

FIFA World Cup: Messi breaks records. The spell remains unbrokenFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi's latest World Cup record is another milestone in a career overflowing with them. Yet the enduring story is not the number itself, but the feeling of watching a footballing genius still at work.

Barcelona target Julian Alvarez wants to leave Atletico Madrid to fulfil his dreamArgentina forward Julian Alvarez has confirmed he wants to leave Atletico Madrid, saying a transfer would help him "fulfil his dream", amid interest from Barcelona, Paris Saint-Germain and Real Madrid.

Mbappe keeps Messi on his toes as France crush Iraq in weather-hit clashKylian Mbappe scored twice as France beat Iraq 3-0 in a World Cup match interrupted by severe weather. The win sent France into the Round of 32 and kept Mbappe within two World Cup goals of Lionel Messi.

France vs Iraq delayed by rain, becomes 1st World Cup match affected by weatherFrance's World Cup game against Iraq was delayed at half-time after thunderstorms closed in on Philadelphia. This was the first time during the tournament that match had to be halted due to adverse weather conditions.

Days after India A's tri-series win, Tilak Varma lights up TG20 with 136 off 56 ballsTilak Varma drove Medak Falcons past Warangal Warriors with an unbeaten 136 in a chase of 259 just days after India A's tri-series win in Sri Lanka. The match turned out to be a run-fest featuring Aman Rao Perala's 142.

FIFA's Messi bias again? Fans spot ignored foul before Argentine's record goalFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi's record-breaking night against Austria should have been remembered purely for history. Instead, a controversial foul in the build-up to his landmark World Cup goal reignited fan claims of favourable officiating towards Argentina and their captain.

Jordan 1-2 Algeria Highlights: Algeria come from behind to knock Jordan outFIFA World Cup, Jordan vs Algeria Highlights: Algeria have taken the lead with another lovely goal from a corner. Striker Al Gouiri heads in the ball past Abulaila and Jordan are on the brink of getting knocked out of the tournament.

The inevitable Messi-ah breaks World Cup record to send Argentina into knockoutsFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi missed an early penalty but still ended the night with two goals and another World Cup record. The Argentina captain became the tournament's all-time leading scorer as the reigning champions booked their place in the knockout rounds.

Messi becomes World Cup's top goalscorer, shatters multiple records on the wayLionel Messi put Argentina ahead against Austria and became the World Cup's all-time top goalscorer. The 38th-minute strike pushed him past Miroslav Klose and added another layer to his record-laden career.

England name squad for T20I series against India, uncapped all-rounder called upEngland have named a 17-player squad for the T20I series against India, with James Coles receiving his first call-up. The selection also brings back Jordan Cox, Sonny Baker and Saqib Mahmood while injuries have ruled out Brydon Carse and Jamie Overton.

Marketa Vondrousova hit with 4-year ban over doping test fiascoFormer Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova has been banned for four years after an independent tribunal ruled against her in an anti-doping test refusal case. The ITIA charged her for allegedly denying a doping officer entry to her home in December 2025.

ARG vs AUT Highlights: Messi double puts Argentina into Round of 32Argentina (ARG) vs Austria (AUT) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi breaks the World Cup record and bags a brace as Argentina move into the Round of 32 with their 2-0 win against Austria.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
